<!--
  Thanks for contributing to Therumunai! Please fill this out completely.
  PR titles MUST follow Conventional Commits (e.g. "feat(report-form): ...") — we
  squash-merge using the PR title and it is linted.
-->

## Summary

<!-- What does this PR do and why? -->

## Related issue

<!-- e.g. Closes #123  (open an issue first for non-trivial changes) -->
Closes #

## Type of change

- [ ] `feat` — new feature
- [ ] `fix` — bug fix
- [ ] `docs` — documentation only
- [ ] `chore` / `ci` / `refactor` / `test` — non-feature change

## Checklist

- [ ] My branch is named `feat/…`, `fix/…`, `docs/…`, or `chore/…`.
- [ ] My commits follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org).
- [ ] I have **signed the [CLA](../docs/CLA.md)** (the CLA check is green).
- [ ] I did **not** commit secrets, PII, `.env` files, or real production infrastructure.
- [ ] Any new user-facing string has **both English and Tamil (தமிழ்)**.
- [ ] The **disclosure footer** is intact on any user-facing view I touched.
- [ ] Out-of-boundary coordinates still fall back to **"Other TN Region"** (if relevant).
- [ ] I added/updated tests and docs where it makes sense.
- [ ] CI passes locally where applicable.

## Notes for reviewers

<!-- Anything that needs extra attention, screenshots, trade-offs, follow-ups. -->
